comments.body: I think your import is wrong. Maybe you could try a star import with import jfxtras.styles.jmetro.*;.

I'm not so sure that the extra repository is needed, JMetro seems to be in Maven central. In any case you never need or should edit your settings.xml, you can declare any extra repository that a project needs directly in the pom itself. maven.apache.org/pom.html#Repositories

I tried running without adding the settings.xml to my ~/.m2/ folder, but it couldn't find the dependency on Maven Central. It seems the creator forgot to add it to Maven Central repo (link here: github.com/JFXtras/jfxtras-styles/issues/…). And I don't think that should have to do anything to this specific issue.
@A-Warm-Jicama it would if the repo is not functioning correctly and gives you a non-functional pom.

answers.body:
The official Maven repo of JMetro didn't work for me as well. I resorted to downloading the JMetro jar from GitHub, adding it manually to Maven, and then use it in my JavaFX apps.

Here's what I did to make JMetro play nice with Maven:

  1. Download the official JMetro jar from GitHub

  2. Add the downloaded jar to the local Maven repo:

    mvn install:install-file -Dfile=jmetro-11.6.15.jar -DgroupId=com.jfxtras -DartifactId=jmetro -Dversion=11.6.15

  3. Add it to the project's pom.xml as a dependency:

    <dependency>
    <groupId>com.jfxtras</groupId>
    <artifactId>jmetro</artifactId>
    <version>11.6.15</version>
</dependency>

  4. For modular projects, declare it in project's module-info.java:

    module <ModuleName> {
    ...
    requires org.jfxtras.styles.jmetro;
    ...
}

Now JMetro can be used normally.

The official Maven repository for JMetro is currently broken, as noted in jfxtras-styles/issues/139.
According to that discussion, the library can still be resolved from JCenter, which I’ve verified to work successfully.

Putting down the details for the same:

Using JMetro from JCenter

Add the JCenter repository as the first entry inside your <repositories> section:

<repository>
    <id>jcenter</id>
    <name>JCenter</name>
    <url>https://jcenter.bintray.com/</url>
</repository>

Note points:

  • JMetro is not compatible with OpenJFX 22 or later. Use OpenJFX 21 or an earlier version.
  • For modular (JPMS) projects, include the following in your module-info.java: requires org.jfxtras.styles.jmetro;

title: Maven: How do I use JMetro styling with Maven?
body:
I wanted to use JMetro styling in my Maven project, but whatever I do, Maven gives me the same error. I am using javafx-archetype-simple from org.openjfx, and I tried using JMetro by adding it as a dependency in my pom.xml, and downloading it from JCenter by adding the .settings.xml to ~/.m2/. Whenever I tried to do mvn javafx:run after mvn clean, I always get this error:

PS: I am really new to using Maven, I would really appreciate some advice regarding using Maven, in general, and also using JavaFX on it. Originally, posted this question on Github Issues, on the JFXtras Styles Git repo, but it wasn't really an issue with JMetro itself, so posted this question here.

Extra info: I don't know if this has anything to do with it, but I am still adding it as reference. When I added JMetro 11.6.7 as a dependency and reran mvn clean javafx:run, while downloading, it gave me these warnings,
